origin: Opening line of a Christian Gospel-style text, specifically the start of the “genealogy of Jesus Christ.” It frames Jesus in relation to Israel’s key patriarchs (Abraham) and royal lineage (David). Most consistent with Matthew 1:1.
author: Likely the author/tradition associated with the Gospel of Matthew (or a Matthew community compiling the text). Could also be a later copyist, but the formula matches Matthew’s authorship tradition.
thoughts: The writer appears to be establishing Jesus’ legitimacy and identity from the start—emphasizing continuity with Jewish history and biblical promises. The motivation is likely to persuade believers that Jesus is the prophesied/expected figure connected to Davidic kingship and Abrahamic covenant.
audience: Early Jewish-Christian or broadly Jewish-background Christian readers who recognize the importance of Abraham (covenant) and David (messianic/royal expectation).
written_when: Most commonly dated to the late 1st century CE (roughly 70–90 CE), consistent with scholarly dating of the Gospel of Matthew.
